# UC-18.1.23 · APIC Controller Resource Exhaustion Watch

## Description

APIC nodes host the policy repository and cluster services; disk, memory, or inode pressure delays policy pushes and can stall fault processing. Watching controller resources prevents brownouts where the fabric stays up but automation and incremental updates fail during change windows.

## Value

APIC nodes host the policy repository and cluster services; disk, memory, or inode pressure delays policy pushes and can stall fault processing. Watching controller resources prevents brownouts where the fabric stays up but automation and incremental updates fail during change windows.

## Implementation

(1) Poll `/api/node/mo/sys/summary` or vendor TA capacity fields every 5 minutes. (2) Alert at staged thresholds; include log partition growth rate. (3) Correlate with cluster replication lag (UC-18.1.18).

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC SNMP or API metrics scripted input.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_aci` `sourcetype="cisco:aci:apic_capacity"` with fields `apic_id`, `disk_used_pct`, `mem_used_pct`, `inode_used_pct`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Poll `/api/node/mo/sys/summary` or vendor TA capacity fields every 5 minutes. (2) Alert at staged thresholds; include log partition growth rate. (3) Correlate with cluster replication lag (UC-18.1.18).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:apic_capacity" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(disk_used_pct) as disk latest(mem_used_pct) as mem latest(inode_used_pct) as inode by apic_id
| where disk>85 OR mem>90 OR inode>85
| eval risk=case(inode>85,"Inode pressure", mem>90,"Memory pressure", disk>85,"Disk pressure",1==1,"OK")
| table apic_id, disk, mem, inode, risk
| sort -mem
```

Understanding this SPL

**APIC Controller Resource Exhaustion Watch** — APIC nodes host the policy repository and cluster services; disk, memory, or inode pressure delays policy pushes and can stall fault processing. Watching controller resources prevents brownouts where the fabric stays up but automation and incremental updates fail during change windows.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_aci` `sourcetype="cisco:aci:apic_capacity"` with fields `apic_id`, `disk_used_pct`, `mem_used_pct`, `inode_used_pct`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC SNMP or API metrics scripted input. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_aci; **sourcetype**: cisco:aci:apic_capacity. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_aci, sourcetype="cisco:aci:apic_capacity", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by apic_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where disk>85 OR mem>90 OR inode>85` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **risk** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **APIC Controller Resource Exhaustion Watch**): table apic_id, disk, mem, inode, risk
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with APIC cluster health and system logs; verify leader, replica, and data-plane sync state in the GUI.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Gauge (per-APIC disk/mem), Table (nodes at risk), Timechart (capacity trends).

## SPL

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:apic_capacity" earliest=-24h
| stats latest(disk_used_pct) as disk latest(mem_used_pct) as mem latest(inode_used_pct) as inode by apic_id
| where disk>85 OR mem>90 OR inode>85
| eval risk=case(inode>85,"Inode pressure", mem>90,"Memory pressure", disk>85,"Disk pressure",1==1,"OK")
| table apic_id, disk, mem, inode, risk
| sort -mem
```

## Visualization

Gauge (per-APIC disk/mem), Table (nodes at risk), Timechart (capacity trends).
